input_or_scenario: Late arrival (Age 16, Anchor 8, Language Use 7, Homophily 5, Density Low, No Institutional Anchor, Transmission 3) in Houston.
hypothesis: Late arrival with strong anchors protects language fluency.
notes: Validates Critical Window theory for late arrivals.

input_or_scenario: Child migrant (Age 9, Anchor 3, Language Use 3, Homophily 2, Density Low, No Institutional Anchor, Transmission 2) in Houston.
hypothesis: Early arrival with weak anchors produces near-complete erosion.
notes: Confirms rapid language attrition in early child migrants.

input_or_scenario: Identical variables (Age 12, Anchor 5, Density Low) run twice — once with Institutional Anchor ON and once with Institutional Anchor OFF.
hypothesis: Institutional anchors protect cultural practice in low-density areas.
notes: Tests the Institutional Buffer effect. All other parameters kept identical across both runs.

input_or_scenario: Same baseline run twice with divergent pivot paths: Community Path (Cultural Youth Group / Founding a Campus Org / Cultural Mentorship) vs. Assimilation Path (The Accent Filter / The Resume Name-Change / Complete Assimilation).
hypothesis: Human choices produce meaningfully divergent trajectories.
notes: Ensures human-in-the-loop choices produce non-cosmetic trajectory shifts.

input_or_scenario: Max structural pressure (Age 5, Anchor 1, Language Use 1, Homophily 1, Density Very Low, No Institutional Anchor, Transmission 1) with Community Anchor pivot applied.
hypothesis: Extreme structural pressure prevents recovery regardless of protective pivot.
success_criteria: Final MR < 45 and Pivot Impact < 8 points
notes: Tests Structural Inertia — environment overrides human agency under extreme conditions.

input_or_scenario: Cross-cultural comparison: Nigerian/Yoruba origin (Lagos) vs. Mexican/Spanish origin (Mexico City) — both arriving in Houston at Age 12 with identical structural variables.
hypothesis: System distinguishes meaningfully between two different cultural origins.
notes: Evaluates system ability to distinguish diaspora groups. Failure reveals Diaspora Generalization bias in LLM training data.

input_or_scenario: Same structural profile run twice: once with a generic biography (empty) and once with a vivid biography (e.g. references to egusi soup and Fela Kuti).
hypothesis: Vivid personal biography improves MR score and sensory narrative quality.
notes: Tests Personalization Fidelity and the influence of qualitative anchors on trajectory.

input_or_scenario: Reflection Agent output evaluated against a 5-point Counterfactual Specificity Rubric: (1) Names a specific alternative city (2) Provides a numeric MR delta (3) Cites a primary driver from the simulation logic (4) Demonstrates causal reasoning (5) Names a real culturally specific institution.
hypothesis: Reflection Agent generates specific and grounded counterfactual scenarios.
notes: Failure indicates the Reflection Agent lacks geographic grounding. Each of the 5 rubric points is scored as 0 or 1.

input_or_scenario: Scenario designed to trigger a >15 point drop in a single life stage (Age 10, Anchor 3, Density Very Low — with assimilation-oriented pivot choices applied).
hypothesis: System detects and flags a tipping-point drop of more than 15 points in one stage.
success_criteria: Binary check: Tipping point detected = True
notes: Verifies escalation logic and crisis event detection. A Volatility Warning badge should surface in the UI.

input_or_scenario: Vivid biography with specific sensory keywords (e.g. egusi soup and Yoruba accent) provided. Drift Agent runs across all four life stages.
hypothesis: Sensory keywords from the personal biography appear in at least two stage narratives.
expected_behavior: sensory keyword count >= 2 across all narrative stages
notes: Tests Narrative Continuity and biography threading. TC-10 failure (count = 0) indicates State Decay — sensory tokens are lost during agent handoff.
